message:
feat: Add playbook management and restore cass/playbook modules

## Restored Modules

### src/playbook.ts (recreated)
Full playbook management module with:
- loadPlaybook()/savePlaybook() with atomic writes
- loadMergedPlaybook() for cascaded global+repo loading
- addBullet()/deprecateBullet() for CRUD operations
- Toxic content filtering via semantic similarity
- Backup creation for corrupted files

### src/cass.ts (updated)
- Simplified cassAvailable() to use --version flag
- Renamed CassSearchHit → CassHit for consistency
- Removed unused warn import

## New CLI Commands

### src/commands/init.ts
Full initialization workflow:
- Creates ~/.cass-memory/ directory structure
- Generates default config.json
- Creates empty playbook.yaml
- Verifies cass CLI availability

### src/commands/playbook.ts
Playbook management subcommands:
- list: Show all active bullets with scores
- show <id>: Display bullet details
- add: Interactive bullet creation
- deprecate <id>: Mark bullet as deprecated
- Supports --json output for scripting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bb9","title":"[Cass] findUnprocessedSessions - Get sessions not yet reflected on","description":"Implement findUnprocessedSessions() to get sessions not yet reflected on.

## Signature
```typescript
interface ProcessedLog {
  processedSessions: Set<string>;
  lastProcessedAt?: string;
}

async function findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog: ProcessedLog,
  options: { days?: number; agents?: string[] },
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string[]>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Call cassTimeline(days) to get recent sessions
2. Extract all session paths from timeline groups
3. Filter out paths already in processedLog.processedSessions
4. Optionally filter by agent names
5. Return unprocessed session paths

## Error Handling
- Return empty array if timeline fails
- Handle missing timeline gracefully
- Log filtering progress at debug level

## Parameters
- processedLog: Tracks which sessions have been reflected on
- options.days: How far back to look (default: 7)
- options.agents: Filter by agent names (e.g., ["claude-code"])

## Algorithm
```typescript
const timeline = await cassTimeline(options.days || 7);
const allSessions = timeline.groups.flatMap(g => 
  g.sessions.map(s => s.path)
);

let unprocessed = allSessions.filter(path => 
  \!processedLog.processedSessions.has(path)
);

if (options.agents) {
  unprocessed = unprocessed.filter(path => {
    const session = timeline.groups
      .flatMap(g => g.sessions)
      .find(s => s.path === path);
    return options.agents.includes(session?.agent);
  });
}

return unprocessed;
```

## Usage
Core function for reflect command:
```typescript
const processedLog = loadProcessedLog();
const unprocessed = await findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog, 
  { days: 7, agents: ["claude-code"] }
);

for (const sessionPath of unprocessed) {
  await reflectOnSession(sessionPath);
  processedLog.processedSessions.add(sessionPath);
}

saveProcessedLog(processedLog);
```","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.582356-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:01.561059-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","title":"[Utils] hashContent - Normalized content hashing for deduplication","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function hashContent(content: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Normalize content:
  - Convert to lowercase
  - Collapse multiple whitespace to single space
  - Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Create SHA-256 hash using crypto.createHash('sha256')
- Update hash with normalized content
- Return first 16 characters of hex digest

## Why This Matters
Critical for exact duplicate detection in curator. Normalization ensures "Test Content" and "test  content" produce same hash, catching duplicates that differ only in formatting. Used in deduplication pass before semantic similarity check.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty string - returns hash of empty string
- Only whitespace - normalized to empty, returns hash of empty
- Very long content - SHA-256 handles any size
- Unicode characters - ensure proper encoding (UTF-8)
- Newlines and tabs - collapsed to single space by normalization","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-bcj","title":"[Generator] executeSearchPlan - Parallel cass searches","description":"Parallel execution of cass searches with deduplication.

SIGNATURE:
async function executeSearchPlan(queries: string[], config: Config): Promise<CassHit[]>

PURPOSE:
Execute all generated queries against cass in parallel, combine results, deduplicate, and return top hits sorted by relevance score.

FLOW:
1. Map queries to cass search promises with config.sessionLookbackDays
2. Execute all with Promise.all (parallel for speed)
3. Flatten results into single array
4. Deduplicate by source_path + line_number (same snippet from multiple queries)
5. Sort by relevance score descending
6. Return top N results (config.maxHistorySnippets or default 10)

CASS SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- limit: 5 per query
- days: config.sessionLookbackDays (default 90)
- agent: config.preferredAgent (optional filter)

DEDUPLICATION LOGIC:
Two hits are duplicates if:
  hit1.source_path === hit2.source_path && hit1.line_number === hit2.line_number
Keep the one with higher relevance score.

RESULT SHAPE:
Array<{
  session_path: string,
  agent: string,
  snippet: string,
  relevance: number,
  source_path: string,
  line_number: number
}>

ERROR HANDLING:
- If cass binary not found, throw error (caught by generateTaskContext)
- If individual query fails, log warning but continue with other queries
- Return empty array if all queries fail","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.025102-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:19.441509-05:00"}

SIGNATURE:
async function executeSearchPlan(queries: string[], config: Config): Promise<CassHit[]>

Execute all generated queries against cass in parallel, combine results, deduplicate, and return top hits sorted by relevance score.

FLOW:
1. Map queries to cass search promises with config.sessionLookbackDays
2. Execute all with Promise.all (parallel for speed)
3. Flatten results into single array
4. Deduplicate by source_path + line_number (same snippet from multiple queries)
5. Sort by relevance score descending
6. Return top N results (config.maxHistorySnippets or default 10)

CASS SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- limit: 5 per query
- days: config.sessionLookbackDays (default 90)
- agent: config.preferredAgent (optional filter)

DEDUPLICATION LOGIC:
Two hits are duplicates if:
  hit1.source_path === hit2.source_path && hit1.line_number === hit2.line_number
Keep the one with higher relevance score.

RESULT SHAPE:
Array<{
  session_path: string,
  agent: string,
  snippet: string,
  relevance: number,
  source_path: string,
  line_number: number
}>

ERROR HANDLING:
- If cass binary not found, throw error (caught by generateTaskContext)
- If individual query fails, log warning but continue with other queries
- Return empty array if all queries fail","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.025102-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:44.041487-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:59:33.686488-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-be2","title":"[Decay] analyzeScoreDistribution - Playbook health metrics","description":"Analyze the distribution of effective scores across all bullets in a playbook. Provides health metrics showing how many rules are excellent, good, neutral, or at-risk. Used by stats command to show playbook quality.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Analyze score distribution across all bullets.
 * Provides playbook health metrics and identifies problematic rules.
 */
function analyzeScoreDistribution(bullets: PlaybookBullet[]): {
  excellent: number;      // score > 10
  good: number;          // score 5-10
  neutral: number;       // score 0-5
  atRisk: number;        // score < 0
  distribution: number[]; // histogram buckets
  avgScore: number;
  medianScore: number;
  totalBullets: number;
} {
  const scores = bullets.map(b => getEffectiveScore(b));
  
  // Category counts
  const excellent = scores.filter(s => s > 10).length;
  const good = scores.filter(s => s > 5 && s <= 10).length;
  const neutral = scores.filter(s => s >= 0 && s <= 5).length;
  const atRisk = scores.filter(s => s < 0).length;

  // Histogram: buckets from -5 to 15 in steps of 2
  const buckets = Array(11).fill(0);
  scores.forEach(score => {
    const bucketIndex = Math.min(10, Math.max(0, Math.floor((score + 5) / 2)));
    buckets[bucketIndex]++;
  });

  // Statistics
  const avgScore = scores.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s, 0) / scores.length;
  const sorted = [...scores].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const medianScore = scores.length % 2 === 0
    ? (sorted[scores.length / 2 - 1] + sorted[scores.length / 2]) / 2
    : sorted[Math.floor(scores.length / 2)];

  return {
    excellent,
    good,
    neutral,
    atRisk,
    distribution: buckets,
    avgScore: Math.round(avgScore * 100) / 100,
    medianScore: Math.round(medianScore * 100) / 100,
    totalBullets: bullets.length
  };
}
```

SCORE CATEGORIES:

Excellent (score > 10):
- Highly proven rules with strong positive signal
- Typically proven maturity with 8+ helpful, 0 harmful
- Core playbook rules that define best practices

Good (score 5-10):
- Solid established or proven rules
- Consistent positive feedback
- Reliable guidance with few issues

Neutral (score 0-5):
- New candidates or low-feedback rules
- Neither strongly positive nor negative
- May need more time to prove value

At Risk (score < 0):
- Rules with harmful marks outweighing helpful
- Candidates for demotion or deprecation
- Need review - may be toxic or obsolete

HISTOGRAM BUCKETS:
```
Bucket 0: score < -5
Bucket 1: -5 to -3
Bucket 2: -3 to -1
Bucket 3: -1 to 1
Bucket 4: 1 to 3
Bucket 5: 3 to 5
Bucket 6: 5 to 7
Bucket 7: 7 to 9
Bucket 8: 9 to 11
Bucket 9: 11 to 13
Bucket 10: score > 13
```

USAGE IN STATS COMMAND:
```typescript
const analysis = analyzeScoreDistribution(playbook.bullets);

console.log('📊 Playbook Health Report');
console.log(`Total rules: ${analysis.totalBullets}`);
console.log(`Excellent (>10): ${analysis.excellent} (${pct(analysis.excellent)}%)`);
console.log(`Good (5-10): ${analysis.good} (${pct(analysis.good)}%)`);
console.log(`Neutral (0-5): ${analysis.neutral} (${pct(analysis.neutral)}%)`);
console.log(`At Risk (<0): ${analysis.atRisk} (${pct(analysis.atRisk)}%)`);
console.log(`Average score: ${analysis.avgScore}`);
console.log(`Median score: ${analysis.medianScore}`);

// ASCII histogram
console.log('\nScore Distribution:');
analysis.distribution.forEach((count, i) => {
  const scoreRange = `${i*2-5} to ${i*2-3}`;
  const bar = '█'.repeat(Math.floor(count / 2));
  console.log(`${scoreRange.padEnd(10)} ${bar} (${count})`);
});
```

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
```
📊 Playbook Health Report
Total rules: 42
Excellent (>10): 8 (19%)
Good (5-10): 15 (36%)
Neutral (0-5): 16 (38%)
At Risk (<0): 3 (7%)
Average score: 6.23
Median score: 5.50

Score Distribution:
-5 to -3   ██ (2)
-3 to -1   █ (1)
-1 to 1    ████ (4)
1 to 3     ████████ (8)
3 to 5     ██████ (6)
5 to 7     ██████████ (10)
7 to 9     ████ (4)
9 to 11    ████ (4)
11 to 13   ██ (2)
>13        █ (1)
```

HEALTH INDICATORS:

Healthy Playbook:
- 60%+ in good/excellent
- <10% at risk
- Median score > 5
- Distribution skews right (positive)

Warning Signs:
- 20%+ at risk
- Median score < 2
- Many rules in -3 to 1 range
- Few or no excellent rules

Critical Issues:
- 50%+ at risk
- Average score < 0
- Distribution skews left (negative)
- Indicates playbook decay or mismatch with codebase

DEPENDENCIES:
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet array

USED BY:
- stats command
- Playbook health dashboard (future)
- Automated alerts (future)
- Quality metrics API (future)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:59.865436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:43.927482-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bjz","title":"[LLM] llmWithRetry - Exponential backoff retry wrapper","description":"Function signature:
async function llmWithRetry<T>(
  operation: () => Promise<T>,
  operationName: string
): Promise<T>

Configuration:
- maxRetries: 3
- baseDelayMs: 1000
- maxDelayMs: 30000
- retryableErrors: ['rate_limit_exceeded', 'server_error', 'timeout', 'overloaded']

Logic:
1. Try operation
2. If error thrown:
   a. Check if error type is retryable
   b. If yes and retries < maxRetries:
      - Calculate delay: min(baseDelayMs * 2^attempt, maxDelayMs)
      - Log retry attempt with delay
      - Wait
      - Retry operation
   c. If not retryable or max retries exceeded, throw error
3. Return result on success

Logging example:
'LLM operation extractDiary failed (rate_limit_exceeded). Retrying in 2000ms... (attempt 2/3)'

Use this wrapper around all LLM operations for resilience against transient failures.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.625399-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:08.984546-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bm7","title":"[Reflector] getIterationNote - Context for subsequent iterations","description":"function getIterationNote(iteration: number): string

- iteration 0: empty string
- iteration 1+: "This is iteration {N+1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

PURPOSE:
Helps LLM find additional insights on subsequent passes

EXAMPLES:
- iteration 0: "" (first pass is broad)
- iteration 1: "This is iteration 2. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."
- iteration 2: "This is iteration 3. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

WHY THIS WORKS:
- Iteration 0: LLM finds obvious patterns
- Iteration 1+: Prompt guides LLM to look deeper
- Specific guidance: "edge cases, anti-patterns, subtle preferences"

IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
if (iteration === 0) return ''
return `This is iteration ${iteration + 1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences.`
```

Used in buildReflectorPrompt()","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.16202-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:00.18406-05:00"}
@@ -184,7 +184,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-hfi","title":"[Cass] withCassErrorRecovery - Generic error recovery wrapper","description":"Implement withCassErrorRecovery() generic wrapper for consistent error handling.

## Signature
```typescript
async function withCassErrorRecovery<T>(
  operation: () => Promise<T>,
  fallback: T,
  options?: { 
    maxRetries?: number; 
    rebuildIndexOnError?: boolean;
  }
): Promise<T>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Try operation
2. On error, check exit code against CASS_EXIT_CODES
3. If retryable and has recovery action, attempt recovery:
   - Exit 3: Rebuild index, retry operation
   - Exit 10: Modify operation params (caller responsibility), retry
4. If max retries exhausted or non-retryable, return fallback
5. Log all recovery attempts

## Error Handling Strategy
```typescript
let attempts = 0;
const maxRetries = options?.maxRetries || 1;

while (attempts <= maxRetries) {
  try {
    return await operation();
  } catch (error) {
    const exitCode = extractExitCode(error);
    const codeInfo = CASS_EXIT_CODES[exitCode];
    
    if (!codeInfo?.retryable || attempts >= maxRetries) {
      log.debug("Non-retryable or max retries, returning fallback");
      return fallback;
    }
    
    // Attempt recovery
    if (codeInfo.action === "rebuild_index" && options?.rebuildIndexOnError) {
      await cassIndex({ full: true });
    }
    
    attempts++;
  }
}

return fallback;
```

## Parameters
- operation: Async function to wrap
- fallback: Value to return on failure
- options.maxRetries: Max retry attempts (default: 1)
- options.rebuildIndexOnError: Auto-rebuild on exit 3 (default: false)

## Usage
DRY wrapper for consistent error handling:
```typescript
// Simple usage
const hits = await withCassErrorRecovery(
  () => cassSearch("query"),
  [],
  { rebuildIndexOnError: true }
);

// Complex usage
const result = await withCassErrorRecovery(
  async () => {
    const timeline = await cassTimeline(7);
    return processTimeline(timeline);
  },
  { groups: [] },
  { maxRetries: 2 }
);
```

## Benefits
- Centralized retry logic
- Consistent error handling across all cass operations
- Automatic recovery actions based on exit codes
- Reduces boilerplate in wrapper functions","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:05.553389-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.203271-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:01.860783-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-hh3","title":"[Curator] handleHelpfulDelta - Record positive feedback","description":"Record positive feedback and check for maturity promotions.

Signature:
function handleHelpfulDelta(
  playbook: Playbook,
  delta: HelpfulDelta
): { applied: boolean; promotion?: PromotionReport }

Process:
1. Find bullet by ID
   - If not found: return applied: false
2. Create FeedbackEvent:
   - timestamp: Date.now()
   - sessionId: delta.sessionId
   - context: delta.context (optional)
3. Push to bullet.helpfulEvents array
4. Increment bullet.helpfulCount
5. Update bullet.lastValidatedAt to current timestamp
6. Update bullet.updatedAt
7. Check for promotion eligibility:
   - candidate → validated: helpfulCount >= 3 && effectiveScore >= 2
   - validated → trusted: helpfulCount >= 10 && effectiveScore >= 8
   - trusted → core: helpfulCount >= 25 && effectiveScore >= 20
8. If promoted:
   - Update bullet.maturity
   - Create PromotionReport
   - Add to result.promotions
9. Return result

Promotion thresholds enforce evidence-based trust. Bullets must prove value over time.

Note: effectiveScore = helpfulCount - harmfulCount","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.952849-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:48.381271-05:00"}

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-hir","title":"[Generator] generateSearchPlan - LLM query generation","description":"LLM-powered query generation for cass searches.

SIGNATURE:
async function generateSearchPlan(task: string, config: Config): Promise<{ queries: string[]; keywords: string[] }>

PURPOSE:
Uses LLM to analyze the task and generate targeted search queries for cass, plus keywords for playbook bullet matching. This ensures we find the most relevant historical context.

OUTPUT:
- queries: 3-5 targeted cass search strings
- keywords: List of important terms for bullet scoring

EXAMPLE:
Task: 'Fix authentication timeout bug'
Output:
  queries: ['authentication timeout', 'token expiry', 'session timeout fix']
  keywords: ['authentication', 'timeout', 'token', 'session', 'jwt']

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Uses config.llmProvider and config.llmModel
- Prompt should emphasize: specific technical terms, error patterns, component names
- Fallback to extractKeywords (simple regex) if LLM unavailable
- Cache queries if same task seen recently (optional optimization)

PROMPT STRUCTURE:
Given task: {task}
Generate 3-5 search queries that would find relevant past work on:
- Similar problems solved
- Related error patterns
- Component/domain mentions
Also extract key technical terms as keywords.

LLM CONFIG:
- Temperature: 0.3 (focused, deterministic)
- Max tokens: 200 (short structured output)
- Format: JSON { queries: [], keywords: [] }","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:53.078843-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:18.723657-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-hpzx","title":"[CLI:doctor] checkStorage - Verify storage health","description":"Implements the checkStorage function that verifies storage health and configuration.

FUNCTION SIGNATURE:
```typescript
async function checkStorage(config: Config): Promise<HealthCheck[]>
```

CHECKS PERFORMED:

1. DATA DIRECTORY:
   - Check if data directory exists
   - Verify write permissions
   - Test by creating temp file
   - Status: FAIL if missing/unwritable, PASS otherwise
   - Fixable: Create directory if missing

2. DISK SPACE:
   - Check available disk space at data directory
   - Warn if <100MB available
   - Fail if <10MB (critical)
   - Status: WARN or FAIL based on threshold
   - Not fixable (user must free space)

3. PLAYBOOK FILE:
   - Check if playbook.json exists
   - Validate JSON structure
   - Test loading and parsing
   - Check for corruption (malformed JSON)
   - Status: WARN if missing (will create), FAIL if corrupted
   - Fixable: Create empty playbook if missing

4. DIARY:
   - Check if diary directory exists
   - Count diary entries
   - Calculate total size
   - Report statistics
   - Status: PASS or WARN (info only)

5. EMBEDDING CACHE:
   - Check if cache directory exists
   - Check cache size and entry count
   - Warn if cache very large (>1GB)
   - Report hit/miss ratio if available
   - Status: PASS or WARN (info only)

IMPLEMENTATION APPROACH:
- Check data directory exists and is writable
- Measure available disk space with threshold checks
- Validate playbook JSON structure and integrity
- Count diary entries and calculate storage usage
- Check embedding cache status and size

ERROR HANDLING:
- Handle missing directories gracefully
- Distinguish ENOENT vs EACCES vs corruption
- Timeout on slow disk operations (10s max)
- Provide specific error messages

DEPENDENCIES:
- fs/promises (file operations)
- diskSpace or df (disk space check)
- Config system
- Path utilities

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Accurately detects all storage issues
- Write test doesn't leave artifacts
- Corruption detection reliable
- Performance: all checks <1 second
- Auto-fix safe (doesn't overwrite existing data)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:55:27.661082-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:55:27.661082-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-hs01","title":"[Config] mergePlaybooks - Combine two playbooks","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
function mergePlaybooks(
  base: Playbook,
  overlay: Playbook | null,
  options: {
    conflictResolution: 'overlay-wins' | 'base-wins';
    deduplication: 'by-id' | 'by-content-similarity';
  }
): Playbook
```

Algorithm:
1. If overlay is null, return base unchanged
2. Merge bullets arrays:
   - Create map by bullet ID
   - Apply conflict resolution for duplicate IDs
   - If deduplication by content-similarity, check semantic similarity (>0.9)
3. Merge deprecated patterns arrays (union)
4. Merge metadata:
   - totalUsageCount: sum both counts
   - lastUsed: max of both timestamps

Deduplication strategies:
- by-id: Only merge bullets with same ID
- by-content-similarity: Also dedupe semantically similar bullets (embedding cosine >0.9)

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-hw7","title":"[Curator] curatePlaybook - Main deterministic merge function","description":"Implement the main deterministic playbook curation function. This is the ACE core - NO LLM calls allowed.

Signature:
function curatePlaybook(
  playbook: Playbook,
  deltas: PlaybookDelta[],
  config: { dedupThreshold: number; pruneThreshold: number; toxicLog?: ToxicEntry[] }
): CurationResult

High-level algorithm:
1. Build hash cache from existing bullets (buildHashCache)
2. Initialize result tracking (applied, skipped, conflicts, etc.)
3. Process each delta by type:
   - AddDelta → handleAddDelta
   - HelpfulDelta → handleHelpfulDelta
   - HarmfulDelta → handleHarmfulDelta
   - ReplaceDelta → handleReplaceDelta
   - DeprecateDelta → handleDeprecateDelta
   - MergeDelta → handleMergeDelta
4. Post-processing phase:
   - Auto-invert harmful bullets (autoInvertHarmfulBullets)
   - Check for maturity transitions (checkPromotions)
   - Prune deprecated bullets (pruneDeprecated)
5. Return comprehensive CurationResult

CRITICAL: This function is DETERMINISTIC. Same inputs = same outputs. No AI inference, no randomness.

This prevents context collapse by ensuring playbook evolution is transparent and reproducible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:55.303034-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:48.795684-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-m2jt","title":"[Semantic] loadOrComputeEmbeddings - Cache-aware embedding","description":"Load embeddings from cache or compute fresh if needed.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function loadOrComputeEmbeddings(
  playbook: Playbook,
  cache: EmbeddingCache
): Promise<void>
```

Process for each bullet:
1. Compute content hash
2. Check cache for bullet.id
3. If cached AND hash matches:
   - Use cached embedding
   - Skip computation
4. Otherwise:
   - Compute fresh embedding
   - Update cache entry
5. Mutate bullet.embedding in place
6. Save cache at end

Cache hit rate:
- First run: 0% (cold cache)
- Subsequent runs: ~95% (only changed bullets recomputed)
- After content change: ~90% (only affected bullets)

Performance:
- Cache hit: <1ms (array lookup)
- Cache miss: ~3ms (embedding computation)
- 100 bullets, 95% hit rate: ~65ms total

Error handling:
- Missing cache file → create new
- Corrupt cache → rebuild
- Hash mismatch → recompute
- Model mismatch → recompute all

Atomic updates:
- Write to temp file
- Move to final location
- Prevents corruption from crashes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:33.888832-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:56.802359-05:00","comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-m2jt","author":"jemanuel","text":"Load embeddings from cache or compute fresh if needed.

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-m55","title":"[Generator] scorePlaybookBullets - Relevance scoring","description":"Score and rank playbook bullets by relevance to task.

SIGNATURE:
function scorePlaybookBullets(bullets: PlaybookBullet[], keywords: string[], config: Config): ScoredBullet[]

PURPOSE:
Calculate relevance scores for playbook bullets to surface the most useful rules for the current task. Combines keyword matching with temporal decay from feedback events.

SCORING ALGORITHM:
For each active bullet:
1. Calculate keyword relevance: scoreBulletRelevance(bullet, keywords)
   - Count keyword matches in content/category/tags
   - Weight by keyword importance (exact match > partial > stem)
   - Range: 0.0 to 1.0

2. Calculate effective score: getEffectiveScore(bullet, config)
   - Base score from helpful/unhelpful events
   - Apply temporal decay (recent feedback weighted higher)
   - Range: -5.0 to +5.0

3. Combine scores: final = relevance * max(0.1, effectiveScore)
   - relevance gates the effective score
   - Minimum 0.1 multiplier prevents total zero-out
   - This ensures keyword match is required for high ranking

4. Sort by final score descending

5. Return top config.maxBulletsInContext (default 10)

FILTERING:
- Only include bullets with status: 'active'
- Exclude bullets marked as deprecated
- Exclude anti-patterns (handled separately)

OUTPUT:
Array<{
  ...bullet,
  relevanceScore: number,
  effectiveScore: number,
  finalScore: number
}>

EDGE CASES:
- No keywords: Use base effective score only
- New bullets (no feedback): Default effective score 0.5
- Negative effective score: Can happen if unhelpful > helpful","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.91684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:20.354512-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-mcs8","title":"[CLI:playbook] export/import - Playbook portability","description":"Export and import playbook for portability and sharing.

Command signatures:
```bash
cass-memory playbook export [--yaml|--json] > playbook.yaml
cass-memory playbook import <file>
```

EXPORT implementation:
1. Load current playbook
2. Serialize to requested format:
   - Default: YAML (more human-readable)
   - --json: JSON format
3. Output to stdout (user redirects to file)

Export format considerations:
- Include all bullets (even deprecated if using --all)
- Strip source session paths (not portable)
- Preserve IDs for reimport
- Include metadata: version, exported_at

IMPORT implementation:
1. Parse input file (detect YAML vs JSON)
2. Validate schema
3. Merge strategy:
   - If bullet ID exists: skip (don't overwrite)
   - If new: add to playbook
   - Optional --replace flag to overwrite existing
4. Display import summary:
   ```
   Imported playbook from playbook.yaml
   - 5 bullets added
   - 2 bullets skipped (already exist)
   - 0 bullets updated
   ```

Use cases:
- Share playbook between team members
- Backup playbook
- Migrate between systems
- Import external best practices

Example workflow:
```bash
# Export on machine A
cass-memory playbook export > my-playbook.yaml

# Import on machine B
cass-memory playbook import my-playbook.yaml

# Import with overwrite
cass-memory playbook import --replace my-playbook.yaml
```

Validation:
- Check file exists
- Validate YAML/JSON syntax
- Validate bullet schema
- Warn on version mismatches","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:38.414909-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:52:38.414909-05:00"}

interface CostEntry {
  timestamp: string;           // ISO 8601

  model: string;               // 'claude-opus-4', 'gpt-4', etc.
  inputTokens: number;
  outputTokens: number;
  estimatedCost: number;       // USD
}

interface MonthlyCost {
  entries: CostEntry[];
  totals: {
    inputTokens: number;
    outputTokens: number;
    estimatedCost: number;
  };
}
```

Operations:
- diary: Diary entry generation
- reflect: Reflection analysis
- validate: Delta validation
- context: Context building/filtering

Implementation:
- One file per month (rotate monthly)
- Append entries as operations complete
- Recalculate totals on save
- Use current pricing from config","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:10.870267-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:10.870267-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qbdq","title":"[Critical] Zero-Config Quick Start Mode - Immediate Value Without Setup","description":"# Zero-Config Quick Start Mode

## The Problem
Current plan requires: init → config → reflect → wait for sessions → curate → then finally get value.
This is too many steps before users see ANY benefit. Most will abandon before step 3.

## The Solution
Make `cm context "my task"` work IMMEDIATELY, even with zero setup:

### Day 1 (No Setup Required):
```bash
# User just runs this - no init, no config
cm context "fix the auth bug"

# Output (even with empty playbook):
# Checking your recent agent sessions...
# Found 3 relevant sessions from Claude Code and Cursor
# 
# ## Relevant Past Work:
# - Session from 2 hours ago: Fixed JWT validation (similar: auth)
# - Session from yesterday: Updated user model schema
# 
# No playbook rules yet. Run 'cm reflect' to start learning from your sessions.
```

### How It Works:
1. Auto-detect ~/.cass directory (create if needed with sensible defaults)
2. Auto-find cass sessions in standard locations
3. Show value immediately from raw cass history
4. Gradually introduce playbook as it gets populated

### Config Auto-Detection:
1. Check ~/.cass-memory/config.json → use if exists
2. Check ./.cass/config.json → use if exists
3. Otherwise → use sensible defaults, no file needed

## Why This Is Critical
- Users need to see value in < 30 seconds
- If first experience is "run init, then configure, then..." they leave
- cass history search alone is valuable - don't hide it behind setup
- Playbook is a bonus, not a prerequisite

## Success Metrics
- Time to first useful output: < 5 seconds
- Required setup steps: 0
- Commands needed for basic usage: 1 (`cm context "task"`)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:05.870433-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:56.287174-05:00","comments":[{"id":27,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-qbdq","author":"jemanuel","text":"CRITICAL FOR USER ADOPTION - This is the #1 priority!

## Problem
Current flow requires too many steps before users see value. Users will abandon by step 5.

## Solution
Zero-config mode that provides value IMMEDIATELY using local keyword extraction + cass search (NO LLM required).

## Acceptance Criteria
- context command works without any configuration
- Works without API keys
- Returns useful results from cass history alone
- Clear messaging about "quick-start mode"

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qef","title":"[Decay] isStale - Detect rules without recent feedback","description":"Detect rules that haven't received any feedback (helpful or harmful) in a configurable period. Stale rules need review - they may be obsolete or just unused.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Check if bullet is stale (no recent feedback).
 * Stale rules may be obsolete, irrelevant, or just not being invoked.
 */
function isStale(bullet: PlaybookBullet, staleDays: number = 90): boolean {
  const allEvents = [...bullet.helpfulEvents, ...bullet.harmfulEvents];
  
  if (allEvents.length === 0) {
    // No feedback ever - check creation date
    return daysSince(bullet.createdAt) > staleDays;
  }

  const lastFeedback = Math.max(
    ...allEvents.map(e => new Date(e.timestamp).getTime())
  );
  
  return daysSince(lastFeedback) > staleDays;
}

function daysSince(timestamp: string | number): number {
  const time = typeof timestamp === 'string' 
    ? new Date(timestamp).getTime() 
    : timestamp;
  return (Date.now() - time) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
}
```

STALENESS LOGIC:

Case 1: No feedback ever
- Check days since bullet.createdAt
- If > staleDays, consider stale
- May indicate rule never matches or is too narrow

Case 2: Has feedback history
- Find most recent event (helpful OR harmful)
- If last event > staleDays ago, consider stale
- May indicate codebase evolved past this rule

DEFAULT: 90 days
- Configurable per playbook or system-wide
- Shorter for fast-moving projects
- Longer for stable, mature codebases

WHY STALENESS MATTERS:

STALE \!= BAD:
- Stale rule might be perfect but just not encountered
- Could be edge case that rarely triggers
- Or could be obsolete cruft from old codebase

STALE RULES NEED REVIEW:
- Human review: Is this still relevant?
- If yes: Keep or pin
- If no: Deprecate or delete
- If unsure: Leave as candidate, watch for another 90 days

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:

1. SECURITY RULE (stale but critical):
   - "Never commit AWS credentials"
   - Created 1 year ago
   - 0 feedback (good thing\!)
   - Stale = true but should be pinned
   - Keep forever even without feedback

2. OBSOLETE FRAMEWORK RULE:
   - "Use Angular 1.x controllers"
   - Last helpful 300 days ago
   - Project migrated to React
   - Stale = true, should deprecate

3. EDGE CASE RULE:
   - "Handle leap year in date calculations"
   - Created 200 days ago
   - 0 feedback (hasn't been encountered)
   - Stale = true but may still be valid
   - Review and decide

INTEGRATION:

Used by stats command:
```typescript
const staleCount = bullets.filter(b => isStale(b)).length;
console.log(`Stale rules (>90 days): ${staleCount}`);
```

Used by review command (future):
```typescript
const staleRules = bullets.filter(b => isStale(b) && \!b.pinned);
console.log('Rules needing review:', staleRules);
```

CONFIGURATION:
- Default staleDays: 90
- Override per-playbook in playbook.json
- Override per-invocation: isStale(bullet, 60)

DEPENDENCIES:
- daysSince() helper
- PlaybookBullet.createdAt field
- PlaybookBullet.helpfulEvents array
- PlaybookBullet.harmfulEvents array

RETURNS:
- true: No feedback in staleDays period
- false: Has recent feedback","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:10.631857-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:44.185002-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qm27","title":"[Decay] applyMaturityTransitions - Batch promotion/demotion","description":"Apply promotion/demotion rules across all bullets in a playbook. Called at the end of each curate cycle to automatically advance proven rules and demote toxic ones. Returns summary of all transitions.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Apply maturity transitions across entire playbook.
 * Checks each bullet for promotion/demotion and applies changes.
 * Called at end of curate cycle.
 */
function applyMaturityTransitions(playbook: Playbook): {
  promotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[];
  demotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[];
  autoDeprecated: { bulletId: string; score: number }[];
  unchanged: number;
} {
  const promotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[] = [];
  const demotions: { bulletId: string; from: string; to: string }[] = [];
  const autoDeprecated: { bulletId: string; score: number }[] = [];
  let unchanged = 0;

  playbook.bullets.forEach(bullet => {
    if (bullet.maturity === 'deprecated') {
      unchanged++;
      return;  // Skip already deprecated
    }

    const currentMaturity = bullet.maturity;
    
    // Check for promotion first
    const promotionTarget = checkForPromotion(bullet);
    if (promotionTarget !== currentMaturity) {
      bullet.maturity = promotionTarget;
      promotions.push({
        bulletId: bullet.id,
        from: currentMaturity,
        to: promotionTarget
      });
      return;
    }

    // Check for demotion
    const demotionTarget = checkForDemotion(bullet);
    if (demotionTarget === 'auto-deprecate') {
      const score = getEffectiveScore(bullet);
      bullet.maturity = 'deprecated';
      bullet.deprecatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
      bullet.deprecationReason = `Auto-deprecated due to negative score (${score.toFixed(2)})`;
      autoDeprecated.push({ bulletId: bullet.id, score });
      return;
    }
    
    if (demotionTarget !== currentMaturity) {
      bullet.maturity = demotionTarget;
      demotions.push({
        bulletId: bullet.id,
        from: currentMaturity,
        to: demotionTarget
      });
      return;
    }

    unchanged++;
  });

  return {
    promotions,
    demotions,
    autoDeprecated,
    unchanged
  };
}
```

TRANSITION RULES:

Processing Order:
1. Skip deprecated bullets (already terminal state)
2. Check promotion first (reward positive signals)
3. Check demotion only if no promotion
4. Apply auto-deprecation if score < -3

Why Promotion First?
- Positive signals should override minor negatives
- Prevents flip-flopping between maturity levels
- Rewards consistent helpful marks

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:

Scenario 1: Mass Promotion
```typescript
// After successful sprint with good playbook usage
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 5 (candidate → established)
// demotions: 0
// autoDeprecated: 0
// unchanged: 37
```

Scenario 2: Codebase Evolution
```typescript
// After major refactor, old rules become toxic
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 2 (new patterns emerging)
// demotions: 8 (old patterns harmful)
// autoDeprecated: 3 (severely toxic old rules)
// unchanged: 29
```

Scenario 3: Stable Playbook
```typescript
// Mature playbook with infrequent changes
const result = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);
// Result:
// promotions: 0
// demotions: 0
// autoDeprecated: 0
// unchanged: 42
```

OUTPUT STRUCTURE:

Promotions:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-123",
  "from": "candidate",
  "to": "established"
}
```

Demotions:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-456",
  "from": "proven",
  "to": "established"
}
```

Auto-Deprecated:
```json
{
  "bulletId": "rule-789",
  "score": -5.25
}
```

INTEGRATION WITH CURATE CYCLE:
```typescript
// At end of curate command
async function finalizeCuration(playbook: Playbook) {
  // 1. Save all feedback events
  await savePlaybook(playbook);

  // 2. Apply maturity transitions
  const transitions = applyMaturityTransitions(playbook);

  // 3. Save updated maturities
  await savePlaybook(playbook);

  // 4. Report to user
  if (transitions.promotions.length > 0) {
    console.log(`✓ Promoted ${transitions.promotions.length} rules`);
    transitions.promotions.forEach(p => {
      console.log(`  - ${p.bulletId}: ${p.from} → ${p.to}`);
    });
  }

  if (transitions.demotions.length > 0) {
    console.log(`⚠ Demoted ${transitions.demotions.length} rules`);
    transitions.demotions.forEach(d => {
      console.log(`  - ${d.bulletId}: ${d.from} → ${d.to}`);
    });
  }

  if (transitions.autoDeprecated.length > 0) {
    console.log(`⛔ Auto-deprecated ${transitions.autoDeprecated.length} rules`);
    transitions.autoDeprecated.forEach(a => {
      console.log(`  - ${a.bulletId} (score: ${a.score.toFixed(2)})`);
    });
  }
}
```

SIDE EFFECTS:
- Modifies playbook.bullets[].maturity in place
- Sets deprecatedAt timestamp for auto-deprecated bullets
- Sets deprecationReason for auto-deprecated bullets
- Does NOT save to disk (caller's responsibility)

IDEMPOTENCY:
Safe to call multiple times - already-transitioned bullets won't re-transition unless scores change.

DEPENDENCIES:
- checkForPromotion()
- checkForDemotion()
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet.maturity field
- PlaybookBullet.deprecatedAt field (optional)
- PlaybookBullet.deprecationReason field (optional)

USED BY:
- curate command (end of cycle)
- Batch processing scripts
- Playbook maintenance tools","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:35.351776-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:43.804062-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qoto","title":"[Config] isSemanticallyToxic - Check against toxic log","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function isSemanticallyToxic(
  content: string,
  toxicLog: ToxicEntry[]
): Promise<boolean>
```

Implementation:
1. Compute content hash (SHA256)
2. Check if hash exists in toxicLog entries
3. If not found by hash, compute content embedding
4. Compare embedding against all toxic embeddings using cosine similarity
5. Return true if similarity > 0.85 threshold

ToxicEntry structure:
```typescript
interface ToxicEntry {
  contentHash: string;
  embedding: number[];
  forgottenAt: string;
  reason?: string;
}
```

Used during playbook loading to filter out bullets marked as toxic via `cm forget` command.
Prevents resurrection of harmful rules across sessions.

Performance: Hash check is O(1), embedding comparison is O(n * embedding_dim) where n = toxic entries.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:23.294264-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:18:09.470988-05:00"}
